commit.message:
Implement authentication and authorisation backend (Identity + JWT) (#130)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement authentication backend with Identity models and Cosmos stores

- Add BlendUser, BlendRole, ExternalLoginInfo domain models in Blend.Domain/Identity
- Add CosmosUserStore implementing IUserStore, IUserPasswordStore, IUserEmailStore,
  IUserLoginStore, IUserRoleStore backed by IRepository<BlendUser>
- Add CosmosRoleStore implementing IRoleStore with in-memory predefined roles
- Register IRepository<BlendUser> in CosmosServiceExtensions for the 'users' container
- Add Microsoft.Extensions.Identity.Core 9.0.3 package to Blend.Infrastructure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement complete authentication API layer

- Add RefreshToken domain entity (Blend.Domain)
- Add JwtOptions configuration class
- Add IJwtService/JwtService for JWT access token generation
- Add IEmailService/ConsoleEmailService for password reset emails
- Add IRefreshTokenService/InMemoryRefreshTokenService for refresh tokens
- Add auth DTOs: RegisterRequest, LoginRequest, ForgotPasswordRequest,
  ResetPasswordRequest, AuthResponse
- Add AuthServiceExtensions with AddBlendAuthentication():
  - ASP.NET Core Identity with CosmosUserStore/CosmosRoleStore (no EF)
  - JWT Bearer authentication with configurable options
  - Conditional Google, Facebook, Twitter/X OAuth providers
  - Authorization policies: RequireAuthenticated, RequireAdmin
  - Rate limiting: fixed window 10 req/min per IP on auth endpoints
- Add AuthController with endpoints:
  POST /api/v1/auth/register
  POST /api/v1/auth/login
  POST /api/v1/auth/login/{provider} (external OAuth)
  GET  /api/v1/auth/callback/{provider}
  POST /api/v1/auth/refresh (HttpOnly cookie-based)
  POST /api/v1/auth/logout
  POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password
  POST /api/v1/auth/reset-password
- Update Program.cs: replace placeholder auth with AddBlendAuthentication()
  and add UseRateLimiter() middleware
- Update appsettings.json: expand Jwt section, add Authentication section
  for Google/Facebook/Twitter credentials

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix code review issues: use DateTimeOffset.UtcNow in JwtService and add null guard for redirectUri in ExternalLogin

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: safe token logging + Twitter OAuth claim mapping

- ConsoleEmailService: log email at Info level, token at Debug only to
  prevent accidental exposure in production log aggregators
- AuthServiceExtensions: add full Twitter OnCreatingTicket handler that
  fetches user info and maps id/name/email claims from Twitter v2 API
  response ({ data: { id, name, email } })

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(tests): add unit and integration tests for auth backend

- JwtServiceTests: token claims, expiry, issuer/audience, refresh uniqueness, Admin role
- PasswordPolicyTests: PasswordOptions config values and PasswordHasher hash/verify
- AccountEnumerationTests: controller unit tests verifying identical 401/200 responses prevent user enumeration
- RefreshTokenServiceTests: InMemoryRefreshTokenService create/get/revoke/expiry
- AuthFlowTests: integration tests with InMemoryUserStore replacing CosmosUserStore for register, login, refresh, logout, forgot-password, reset-password flows

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: flatten @Nested tests to avoid empty Surefire XML files

JUnit 5 @Nested classes cause Maven Surefire to emit an empty outer
XML file (tests=0) alongside the nested-class XML files. The Testream
junit-reporter's junit-to-ctrf converter crashes when it encounters a
<testsuite tests="0"> element with no <testcase> children.

Refactor all three test classes to use flat methods instead of @Nested.
Each @DisplayName now prefixes the group name (e.g. 'formatPrice - ...'),
preserving readability while generating a single, well-formed XML file
per class that the reporter can process without errors.

feat: hybrid per-user question dedup for board modes

Replace the global used=true-forever approach with a two-layer strategy:

Layer 1 — used=true as a game-lifetime lock:
- Questions are returned to the pool (used=false) when a game finishes
  or is abandoned, not left permanently consumed.
- All pool_question_ids are now returned on game end, not just unanswered
  ones. user_question_history provides the per-user protection going forward.

Layer 2 — user_question_history for 60-day per-user dedup:
- draw_board() now accepts p_user_ids uuid[] and excludes any question
  already in any participant's history. Drawn questions are atomically
  inserted into user_question_history for all participants.
- When a guest joins an existing online/duel game the host already drew,
  the board's question IDs are recorded in the guest's history via
  record_board_question_history() — no redraw needed.
- Duel createGame/joinQueue pre-fetches host's seen IDs and passes them
  as excludeIds to drawForDuel() to avoid host duplicates.
- cleanup_old_question_history() window extended to 60 days (was 30).
  Daily cleanup cron keeps the table bounded, eliminating the need for
  seen_at filters in queries (pure PK point-lookups, sub-millisecond even
  at 100M rows).

New DB functions (migration 20260414000000):
- draw_board(p_exclude_ids, p_user_ids) — extended signature
- record_board_question_history(user_ids, question_ids) — guest join
- reset_board_history_if_exhausted(user_id) — auto-reset at 80% pool seen
- cleanup_old_question_history() — updated to 60-day window

Backend:
- question-pool.service.ts: drawBoard(excludeNews, allowFallback, userIds)
  + recordBoardHistory() helper
- supabase.service.ts: getSeenQuestionIds() removes inline time filter
- game.types.ts: CreateGameDto gets optional playerIds[]
- game.service.ts: passes playerIds to drawBoard; finishSession returns all
- online-game.service.ts: passes hostId on draw; records guest history on
  join; returns all pool questions on finish/abandon
- duel.service.ts: excludes host seen history on draw; records guest history
  on join; returns all pool questions on abandon

Pool sizing impact: a pool of 5k-10k questions comfortably supports
thousands of active users with no repeats within 60 days.

Cosmos: Add user agent tracking for azure-cosmos-encryption SDK (#48505)

* Cosmos: Add user agent tracking for azure-cosmos-encryption SDK

The azure-cosmos-encryption library wraps the core CosmosAsyncClient
without appending its own identifier to the user agent string, making
it impossible to track encryption SDK adoption through telemetry.

Changes:
- Add azure-cosmos-encryption.properties resource file with
  artifactId and version (Maven-filtered at build time)
- Add USER_AGENT_SUFFIX constant in Constants.java that reads the
  properties via CoreUtils.getProperties() (same pattern as Kafka
  connector)
- Add appendUserAgentSuffix() method to AsyncDocumentClient interface
  and RxDocumentClientImpl to allow appending to the UserAgentContainer
  after client construction
- Call appendUserAgentSuffix() in CosmosEncryptionAsyncClient
  constructor to append 'azure-cosmos-encryption/{version}' to the
  existing user agent

The resulting user agent will look like:
azsdk-java-cosmos/4.x.x OS/ver JRE/ver <customer-suffix> azure-cosmos-encryption/2.x.y

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  and CosmosEncryptionContainer (core SDK API is already public)

feat(api): enforce squad number immutability on PUT (#529)

Add a mismatch guard in put_async: if squad_number in the request
body does not match the path parameter, return HTTP 400 Bad Request.
The path parameter is the authoritative source of identity on PUT.

Document the single-model design decision in PlayerRequestModel:
one model intentionally covers both POST and PUT, with per-operation
differences handled at the route layer.
